_about: Citation-readiness score distribution across one Advocate-enabled tenant (WCC) and six archetype non-customer SMB websites. The WCC score reflects WCC's actual harness output as of generated_at_utc. The non-customer scores reflect anonymized archetypes, a credibility move so the with-Advocate panel doesn't sit alone vs two cherry-picked baselines. Each non-customer business is described by category, not name, since we haven't asked permission to publish their score. To reproduce against your own site: see /methodology.html for the full judge prompt + rubric.

examples.label: Advocate-enabled
examples.subtitle: Workman Copy Co. (Austin, TX)
examples.judge_excerpt: Per-bot rendering with verified ratings, structured services, FAQ schema. AI engines can extract clean entity data. AggregateRating sourced from real Google Maps URL with dateModified hint, no marketing language deductions.

examples.label: No Advocate
examples.subtitle: Mid-size Austin commercial law firm
signals_present: Practice area pages with FAQ markup
examples.judge_excerpt: Solid attorney bios with Person schema and individual practice-area pages. FAQ markup on a few of the bigger pages. Missing per-engine renderer, no Speakable annotations, AggregateRating only via Google's GBP scrape rather than embedded JSON-LD. Citation likely on attorney-name queries; weaker on category-comparison queries.

examples.label: No Advocate
examples.subtitle: Regional HVAC chain (4-8 locations)
examples.judge_excerpt: Multi-location LocalBusiness schema is well-formed and the service area is structured. Emergency-services FAQ helps with intent queries. Reviews are shown as prose testimonials, not Review schema, which limits AggregateRating extraction. Service inventory is generic ('heating, cooling, plumbing') without sub-services.

examples.label: No Advocate
examples.subtitle: Boutique dental practice (single location)
examples.judge_excerpt: Dentist schema is present and well-formed. Insurance acceptance is prose-only, so 'do they take Cigna?' queries can't extract cleanly. Reviews exist on Google but no AggregateRating in the page itself. Speakable on the contact block is unusual and helpful.

examples.label: No Advocate
examples.subtitle: Mid-tier property management firm
examples.judge_excerpt: Basic LocalBusiness schema present. Property listings are pure HTML cards with no structured Product/RealEstateListing markup, so an extractor can't pull individual properties cleanly. About page is prose-heavy with marketing claims unsupported by review schema or third-party citations.

examples.label: No Advocate
examples.subtitle: Generic local florist
examples.judge_excerpt: Generic homepage prose. Missing structured ratings, no per-engine optimization, no clear service inventory. Marketing language without specifics. AI extractor cannot anchor a citation to verifiable facts.

examples.label: No Advocate
examples.subtitle: Sole-proprietor handyman / plumber
examples.judge_excerpt: Minimal site, mostly marketing text and a phone number. No schema, no FAQ, no structured services. Available hours described in prose ('mostly weekdays, weekends by appointment'). Citation only when no better source exists for the query.

harness.notes: WCC is real harness output. Non-customer archetypes are anonymized (category, not business name) since we haven't asked permission to publish specific firms' scores. The methodology page publishes the full judge prompt + rubric so any reader can reproduce against their own site.
